candidates.title: Reduce repetitive refrain-style sentences in fallback prose
candidates.description: Detect repeated closing or payoff sentences across segment nodes and vary them before draft1 is committed.
candidates.detection_hint: Repeated line families such as '결국 이 장이 하려는 일도...' appearing across multiple segment blocks in the same chapter.
candidates.proposed_fix: Normalize repeated patterns into one per section and force alternative closing templates for later segments.
candidates.promotion_policy: Promote to immutable core only after the same repetition pattern is confirmed across at least 3 chapters without harming legitimate emphasis.

candidates.title: Fix Korean particle collisions after quoted titles or phrases
candidates.description: Catch awkward Korean 조사 collisions that appear after quoted expressions or book-level hook phrases.
candidates.detection_hint: Examples like quoted phrase plus duplicated object/topic particle in a single clause.
candidates.proposed_fix: Apply a safe Korean quote-particle cleanup pass before draft1 output is finalized.
candidates.promotion_policy: Promote only after chapter-level review confirms the cleanup is precision-safe across multiple titles and quoted phrases.

candidates.title: Prefer chapter-specific details over generic payoff language
candidates.description: When raw guide contains book-specific motifs, the fallback prose should spend more budget on those motifs instead of repeating general payoff language.
candidates.detection_hint: Segments remain structurally correct but overuse generic reader payoff statements while underusing chapter motifs and chapter goal details.
candidates.proposed_fix: Raise the priority of chapter_goal, include_items, and local notes before generic payoff sentences are emitted.
candidates.promotion_policy: Promote after validation across at least one cinema/history chapter and one travel/taste chapter.
